summary: Harden the generated HTTP cache after PR review (live path + unused scaffolding package).
reason: GET cache responses can contain user-scoped Pushover data, so cache files must be owner-only; the review also identified a redundant AuthHeader read in the cache key path. A later Greptile pass surfaced that the unused internal/cache package wrote at 0o644; aligned it to 0o600 so it doesn't ship a regression vector if it's ever wired up.
validated_outcome: go test ./internal/client -run TestWriteCacheUsesOwnerOnlyPermissions -count=1 confirms live cache files are written 0o600; publish-package verifier passes locally. internal/cache has no callers and no tests; the change is a one-line constant align that go vet ./... and go build ./... cover.
